artifacts.summary:
## Overview
The meeting discussed key review metrics, including community MRs, MR rates, and defect tracking. The team agreed to simplify the metrics and focus on other indicators to improve quality and security. The narrow MR rate will be held at 10 as the target going forward.

## Key Discussion Points
### Community MRs and MR Rates
* The team discussed the difference between RD Overall and RD Wider MR rates.
* It was agreed that RD Wider MR rate captures community contributions only and no internal contributions.
* The team proposed tracking the percentage of total MRs that come from the community over time.
* The proposal was accepted, and Lily will work with Max to make the transition.

### Defect Tracking and SLOs
* The team discussed the defect tracking and SLOs, and it was agreed that there is a first iteration PI to show a percentage of defects meeting the SLOs.
* The key findings showed that S1 and S2 are hovering at 80% and 60%, respectively.
* The team discussed the issue of measuring SLO achievement with closed bugs and agreed to measure the percentage of open bugs that are within SLO.

### SUS (System Usability Scale)
* The team discussed the SUS metrics and noted that there was a decline in Q4, but it was the smallest decline over several quarters.
* It was agreed that the team needs to keep an eye on SUS and not assume that the current trend is a real improvement.

### Security Discussion
* The team discussed the need to prioritize security work and ensure that it is reflected in the metrics.
* It was agreed that the team needs to do long-term work to make sure that security metrics are accurate.

### Rate Discussion
* The team discussed the narrow MR rate, which is significantly below target.
* It was agreed that the team will hold the line at 10 as the target going forward and focus on other indicators to improve quality and security.

## Decisions Made
* The team will track the percentage of total MRs that come from the community over time.
* The narrow MR rate will be held at 10 as the target going forward.
* The team will focus on other indicators to improve quality and security.
* The defect tracking and SLOs will be continued to be improved.
* The team will measure the percentage of open bugs that are within SLO.

## Open Questions
* The team needs to keep an eye on SUS and not assume that the current trend is a real improvement.
* The team needs to prioritize security work and ensure that it is reflected in the metrics.
* The team needs to do long-term work to make sure that security metrics are accurate.
* The team needs to figure out how to measure the percentage of open bugs that are within SLO computationally.
artifacts.followups:
## Email
Subject: Follow-up on Engineering Key Review Meeting

Dear All,

This email serves as a follow-up to our Engineering Key Review meeting on February 18, 2021. We discussed key review metrics, including community MRs, MR rates, and defect tracking. The main decisions and action items from the meeting are:

* We will track the percentage of total MRs that come from the community over time.
* The narrow MR rate will be held at 10 as the target going forward.
* We will focus on other indicators to improve quality and security.
* Defect tracking and SLOs will continue to be improved.

Lily will work with Max to make the transition to tracking the percentage of total MRs that come from the community over time.

Please let me know if you have any questions or concerns regarding these decisions and action items.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

## Slack
We've decided to track the percentage of total MRs that come from the community over time instead of the complex MR rate metric. We'll also hold the narrow MR rate at 10 as the target going forward. Let's focus on other indicators to improve quality and security.
artifacts.brief:
COORDINATION BRIEF

PARTICIPANTS:
- Eric Johnson
- Lily
- Sid
- Max
- Mike
- Craig
- Steve
- Rob
- Ryan
- Christopher
- Mac

SCOPE:
The meeting discussed key review metrics, including community MRs, MR rates, and defect tracking. The goal is to improve visibility, objectivity, and productivity while addressing security and quality concerns.

DECISION CUES:
- The team will track the percentage of total MRs that come from the community over time.
- The narrow MR rate will be held at 10 as the target going forward.
- The team will focus on other indicators to improve quality and security.
- The defect tracking and SLOs will be continued to be improved.

CAUTIONS:
- The team should be cautious not to game the system by producing fewer MRs with engineers at GitLab.
- The metrics should be adjusted to reflect the underlying problem of measuring open bugs and SLOs.
- The team should keep an eye on SUS (System Usability Scale) and not assume that the current trend is a real improvement.
- The team should prioritize security work and ensure that it is reflected in the metrics.
